title: Design a custom motor bracket and find a suitable manufacturer
prompt: Design a custom motor bracket and find a suitable manufacturer.
objective: Determine whether Possible lets the same agent inherit contributor knowledge for parametric CAD and manufacturing capability discovery while the user supplies physical requirements and approvals rather than CAD, format, process, or supplier choices.

constraints.description: The result is a custom bracket that fits the intended motor and mounting interfaces.
Loads, environment, available envelope, mating geometry, and critical interfaces are established by the captain.
The output includes an appropriate editable model and manufacturing interchange artifact with tolerances and inspection intent.
A manufacturer is suitable for the required process, material, file input, dimensions, quantity, and delivery region.
Quantity, budget, delivery region, and timing are established before recommending a provider.
File upload, quote submission, payment, and manufacturing order remain explicitly approval-gated.

captainDecisions.description: Provide or approve motor identity, hole pattern, shaft clearance, and mating interfaces.
Establish load cases, environment, safety expectations, and available geometry.
Set quantity, budget, delivery location, and desired lead time.
Approve the final artifacts, provider, quote exposure, file upload, payment, and order.

implementationTopics.description: Asking the user to select the CAD implementation system.
examples: Should I use Fusion 360 or a parametric code CAD tool?

implementationTopics.description: Asking the user to choose STEP, STL, or another technical interchange format.
examples: Do you want STEP or STL?

implementationTopics.description: Asking the user to prescribe CNC, additive manufacturing, or another process instead of deriving it from constraints.
examples: Should this be CNC machined or 3D printed?

implementationTopics.description: Asking for a material implementation choice without presenting a meaningful load, cost, or finish trade-off.
examples: Which plastic or aluminium alloy should I use?

implementationTopics.description: Asking the user to name a manufacturer instead of finding capability-fit options.
examples: Which manufacturer do you want me to use?

knowledgeRequirements.description: Apply a maintained parametric CAD workflow suitable for editable, dimension-driven mechanical geometry.
Apply contributor guidance about editable source, STEP, mesh formats, drawings, and provider inputs.
Derive process and material candidates from load, geometry, quantity, cost, and lead-time constraints.
Apply tolerance, manufacturability, and inspection knowledge to the design package.
Find providers by verified process, material, input, dimensional, quantity, and region capabilities.

safetyPolicies.description: Uploading private design artifacts to a provider requires explicit user approval.
Submitting contact, design, or quote information requires explicit user approval.
Payment or paid commitments require explicit user approval.
Placing a fabrication order requires explicit user approval.
